title: Polynomial, Rational, and Nonlinear Models · Decision Notebook: Polynomial Shape
summary: Compare alternative growth and geometry models for a production process. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int polynomial shape increment in a nonlinear model comparison report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate polynomial degree, roots, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.
Use sensitivity and shadow interpretations to defend a constrained linear decision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Polynomial Shape evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (2, 8) and (6, 20). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (20 − 8) / (6 − 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (20 − 8) / (6 − 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.
